macro_summary: U.S. equities closed lower Wednesday as hawkish Fed commentary and a new tariff proposal on 60 trading partners over forced labor weighed on sentiment (per Reuters). The S&P 500 slipped 0.7% while tech held up better, with the SOXX semiconductor index rising 1.3% to a fresh record on Computex-driven AI optimism. Oil steadied near $95/bbl as fresh U.S.-Iran clashes threatened already fragile Hormuz reopening talks, keeping energy supply risk elevated ahead of Friday's May nonfarm payrolls report.
commodities_note: WTI edged up 0.1% to $95.23, consolidating after the prior session's surge, as the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed with only a trickle of tanker traffic (per CNN/Kpler). Gold was flat at $4,495 while the 10-year yield ticked up to 4.49% on renewed Fed rate-hike bets. The dollar index was little changed at 99.44.

accumulate_gates.note: All mechanical gates PASS. You MUST assign ACCUMULATE unless a specific ticker-level thesis deterioration exists (see ASSIGNMENT RULE).

writeup.headline: Upgraded to ACCUMULATE — all 8 mechanical gates pass, R:R 2.8:1.

The thesis has two independent legs: non-OpenAI RPO growth (+28%) and in-house AI model development (per Yahoo Finance, Jun 2) that directly addresses the OpenAI concentration guardrail. Entry zone $427–432, invalidation $405.62. Half position — the nearest resistance at $432.26 (1.2% above) must break on volume for the full upside to materialise. ACCUMULATE — starter position. PAPER TRADE — under evaluation.

singapore.summary: All three Singapore banks remain hard-blocked on extension, with DBS at RSI 77, OCBC at RSI 68, and UOB the closest to a reset at RSI 65. NIM compression from elevated US10Y (4.49%) and MAS S$NEER appreciation persists as the structural headwind, now 69 days without relief. No catalyst until the next MAS decision in October, and no divergence within the cluster — all three trade in lockstep with the rate backdrop.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — NIM compression from rates and FX, awaiting US10Y break below 4.20% or MAS policy shift.
singapore.key_development: No new catalyst; the cluster remains overbought and awaits a macro trigger for re-entry.

semiconductors.summary: The semiconductor cluster extended its rally, with SOXX hitting a record 615.68 (+1.3%) on Computex-driven AI optimism. Extreme divergence persists: AMD (+56% above 50-day), MU (+81%), and SK Hynix (+60%) are in deep extension, while NVDA's pullback to +6.3% is the most reasonable setup but still hard-blocked. Intel's 6% surge on Lip-Bu Tan's keynote is hype without 18A external wins; AVGO's post-earnings disappointment underscores the valuation risk when extension meets a soft guide.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extended — AI capex cycle intact, but all 13 watchlist semis are hard-blocked on price, leaving no entries.
semiconductors.key_development: AVGO's Q2 AI revenue guide of $16B fell short of expectations (per Reuters Videos), triggering a post-earnings selloff that should reset the name over the coming sessions.

big_tech.summary: BigTech diverged: Microsoft's pullback to $427 created the only actionable setup in the watchlist, with all 8 ACCUMULATE gates passing. Amazon and Google are on HOLD with momentum warnings and distorted R:R, while Palantir's pullback cleared its extension block but the 69x forward PE and distorted R:R keep it on HOLD. The cluster's AI capex commitment ($5.3T through 2030 per Goldman Sachs) is the structural tailwind, but only MSFT offers a clean entry today.
big_tech.thesis_status: Cautiously constructive — MSFT offers a starter position; AMZN and GOOG need momentum to stabilize before re-evaluation.
big_tech.key_development: Microsoft's in-house AI model launch marks a strategic pivot to reduce OpenAI dependency, directly addressing the credit circularity guardrail.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: Receiving a matching $2B investment from NVIDIA for AI photonics alongside Lumentum — the second leg of the NVDA photonics ecosystem play.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if COHR pulls back 8–10% from current levels on a broader semis consolidation.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC design wins (Amazon Trainium, Google Axion) make it a direct AVGO competitor and a read-through on hyperscaler ASIC spend trajectory.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist after AVGO's post-earnings dust settles, if MRVL holds its 50-day on volume.

interconnected.name: Qualcomm
interconnected.reason: Snapdragon AI edge chips leverage Arm architecture — surfaces as an on-device AI inference play alongside NVDA's data-center buildout and new PC chip push.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if QCOM dips 5–7% on broader tech weakness triggered by NFP or CPI.

active_risks: Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed (day 94), with only a trickle of tanker traffic; fresh U.S.-Iran clashes threaten ceasefire talks (per CNN, World Oil).
Trump administration proposes 10–12.5% tariffs on imports from 60 economies over forced labor failures, reigniting trade-policy uncertainty (per Reuters, Yahoo Finance).
Fed holds rates steady, but hawkish commentary (Hammack, Logan) and elevated inflation (3.8% PCE) keep a December rate hike on the table (per Reuters, Bloomberg).
U.S.-China semiconductor tensions persist: Senators Warren and Kim criticize Trump for allowing AI chip exports to overseas Chinese entities, while China's manufacturing PMI shows slower expansion.
geopolitical.market_impact: Elevated oil (~$95/bbl) props up defense and energy names but squeezes consumer spending and broad market sentiment. The SOXX index hit a record, but all watchlist semis are hard-blocked on extension — the AI capex theme is structurally intact but priced to perfection. Singapore banks remain overbought with NIM compression from elevated US rates and strong SGD.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: The U.S. Trade Representative proposed new tariffs on 60 economies over forced labor, while fresh U.S.-Iran military exchanges raised doubts on Hormuz reopening (per Reuters, CNN).
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Maintain a defensive cash posture — high extension across the watchlist limits entries. Only MSFT offers a starter position today. Wait for post-NFP clarity and the June 10 CPI before committing more capital.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive with pockets of opportunity in AI infrastructure and robotaxi.
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 18 of 27 names are hard-blocked on technical extension; elevated oil and hawkish Fed keep fresh entries scarce. The only actionable ACCUMULATE is MSFT, with WRD on WATCH for a robotaxi catalyst.

accumulate.note: Entry zone $427–432, stop below $405.62. R:R 2.8:1 to $487. Wide-stop R:R 2.1:1 clears the gate. Half position — nearest resistance at $432.26 must break on volume. ACCUMULATE — starter position. PAPER TRADE — under evaluation.

WRD → closer to ACCUMULATE (risk-on for speculative names)

Singapore banks → deeper CAUTION (US10Y above 4.5% extends NIM compression)

calibration_insights.confidence_banner: Actionable buckets BUY (n=4) / ACCUMULATE (n=11) spanning chop, trend_up. NOT yet decision-grade — alpha figures are benchmark-relative; single-regime samples carry no out-of-regime evidence.
calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
